executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups: coinbasecartel and shinyhunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, and National Money Mart with a confirmed database leak. The Qilin ransomware group alone targeted 18 Canadian organizations including the Eastern Townships School Board and Workers Health & Safety Centre. Critical infrastructure including healthcare, education, public sector, and financial services are under active assault by at least 20 distinct ransomware operations.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 18 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Waterloo Information Systems, Kuzco Lighting, Traffic Tech, Service Star Freightways, and multiple other manufacturing, healthcare, and professional services firms
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1048 Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: Clop

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a severe ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure is heavily targeted including hospitals (Markham Stouffville Hospital), public health units (Peterborough), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College), and municipal government (Municipality of North Perth). No CCCS advisories were identified in this collection period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was targeted by two separate ransomware groups (coinbasecartel and shinyhunters), representing dual extortion attempts against the major Canadian retailer. Active Green + Ross (consumer services/automotive retail) was compromised by Sinobi group. Kids & Company (childcare services provider) was also hit by Sinobi.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loan and financial services) suffered a confirmed database leak by the Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers was compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting and financial services firms were hit including Pewarchuk CPA (Play group) and MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin group).

report_2w.executive_summary: Multiple critical vulnerabilities are under active exploitation right now. Citrix NetScaler (CVE-2026-3055) is being exploited within hours of disclosure, prompting a Canadian government alert. Fortinet FortiClient EMS has a critical flaw (CVE-2026-21643) confirmed exploited in attacks. F5 BIG-IP was upgraded from a denial-of-service bug to a critical remote code execution vulnerability now exploited in the wild. The European Commission suffered a major breach by threat actor ShinyHunters, and Russian APT Star Blizzard (TA446) is targeting iPhones with the DarkSword exploit kit. A supply chain attack (TeamPCP) compromised Telnyx SDK to steal cloud credentials, potentially affecting Databricks customers.

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued two critical advisories this reporting period: AL26-006 for Citrix NetScaler (CVE-2026-3055) under active exploitation, and AL26-005 for Microsoft SharePoint Server (CVE-2026-20963). A research study highlighted that smart home breach recovery guidance is falling behind in Canada. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported.
